name: अर्जुनविषादयोग
teaching: Before any teaching can begin, something must break. Arjuna — the finest warrior alive — looks at what his duty actually costs and collapses. The Gita honours his collapse: despair, seen clearly, is the doorway to inquiry.
reflections: Where in your life are you certain you are right, the way Duryodhana is certain?
Arjuna's reasons for not acting sound noble. When have your own noblest-sounding reasons hidden fear?
What would it take for you to set down your bow — to admit, fully, 'I do not know what to do'?

name: सांख्ययोग
teaching: Krishna's first and deepest answer: you grieve over what cannot die, and cling to what cannot stay. Act because action is yours; let go of results because they never were. The chapter ends with a portrait of the person who has actually absorbed this — steady, undisturbed, free.
reflections: What are you currently treating as permanent that is, honestly, passing?
If the results of your work were guaranteed to remain unknown to you, how would you work?
The sthitaprajña withdraws the senses 'as a tortoise draws in its limbs.' What would that look like in one hour of your day?

name: कर्मयोग
teaching: Renouncing the world is not the same as renouncing the ego, and stillness of the body is not stillness of the mind. Krishna closes the escape route: no one stays actionless for even a moment, so act — fully, skillfully, as offering — and let the action itself become the discipline.
reflections: Where are you performing inaction — appearing detached while the mind churns?
Whose example are you, whether you intend it or not?
Krishna names desire as the enemy 'seated in the senses, mind and intellect.' Where is it seated most firmly in you?

name: ज्ञानकर्मसंन्यासयोग
meaning: The Yoga of Knowledge and Action
teaching: Action done as offering stops binding the doer — it burns in the fire of knowledge like fuel. Krishna widens the lens: this teaching is ancient, the teacher returns age after age, and every sincere path of sacrifice ends in the same seeing. Knowledge is the rarest fire, and everything offered into it is purified.
reflections: What, in your daily work, could you genuinely treat as an offering rather than a transaction?
Krishna says the wise see 'action in inaction, and inaction in action.' Sit with one day of your life through that lens.
What doubt, left uncut, is quietly governing your decisions?

name: कर्मसंन्यासयोग
teaching: Asked to choose between renouncing action and performing it, Krishna refuses the choice: both lead to freedom, but acting without attachment is the more honest road. True renunciation is inner — the knower acts, yet holds 'I do nothing at all.' Equanimity, not abstention, is the mark of the free.
reflections: What have you given up outwardly while still gripping it inwardly?
Could you do today's most tedious task as if you were not its doer?
Where do you still divide the world into what elevates you and what is beneath you?

name: ध्यानयोग
teaching: The mind must be befriended before it can be stilled: for the one who has conquered it, the mind is the closest ally; for the one who hasn't, it behaves like an enemy. Krishna gives the practice — seat, posture, moderation, patient return — and the image: a lamp in a windless place, unflickering. Even the practitioner who falls short loses nothing; no sincere effort on this path is ever wasted.
reflections: Right now, is your mind your friend or your opponent?
Where is your life immoderate — in eating, sleeping, effort, rest — in ways that make stillness impossible?
Arjuna admits the mind is as hard to hold as the wind. What is your actual, daily relationship with practice and patience?

name: ज्ञानविज्ञानयोग
meaning: The Yoga of Knowledge and Realisation
teaching: Krishna begins to speak of himself: the taste in water, the light in the moon and sun, the thread on which all worlds are strung like pearls. The divine is not elsewhere — it is the essence inside every essence. Few seek this; of the four kinds who turn to God, the one who seeks knowing itself is dearest, for that one seeks nothing in return.
reflections: When you pray or aspire, which of the four are you — the distressed, the seeker of gain, the curious, or the one who simply wants to know?
Can you taste water today and notice what the taste itself is?
What 'deity' — career, approval, security — do you worship for limited fruit?

name: अक्षरब्रह्मयोग
teaching: What you dwell on, you become — and what fills the mind at the end of life reveals what filled it all along. Krishna speaks plainly about death: the cosmos itself breathes in and out, worlds arise and dissolve, and beyond all of it stands the imperishable, reached by remembrance. Therefore remember now; the final thought is only the sum of every ordinary one.
reflections: If your last thought will be your most practiced thought, what are you practicing?
What in your life belongs to the cycle that returns, and what to that which does not?
Could you pair one daily act — breath, walking, work — with remembrance, starting today?

name: राजविद्याराजगुह्ययोग
teaching: The secret hidden in plain sight: everything rests in the divine the way wind rests in space — held, pervaded, yet never grasping or grasped. No elaborate rite is needed; a leaf, a flower, water, offered with love, is received. And the door is closed to no one — whoever comes, however late, however fallen, arrives.
reflections: What is the smallest thing you could offer today — and could you actually offer it?
Krishna says he is the same to all beings. Where do you assume you are disqualified?
What would change if you believed nothing devotional is ever lost?

name: विभूतियोग
teaching: Wherever you find splendour — in rivers the Ganga, in mountains Meru, in speech the single syllable, in the silent the silence — you are seeing a spark of one fire. Krishna lists his glories not as autobiography but as a training of the eye: learn to trace every excellence back to its source. The whole universe stands on a fragment of it.
reflections: What most recently stopped you with its beauty or power — and did you follow it back to its source?
Among the glories Krishna names are gambling of the dishonest and the rod of punishers. Can you find the sacred even in what disturbs you?
If you listed your own ten glories, what would the list reveal about what you worship?

name: विश्वरूपदर्शनयोग
meaning: The Vision of the Cosmic Form
teaching: Arjuna asks to see — and is given eyes no mortal has. The universal form is not a comfort: it is all faces, all fires, all futures, armies streaming into blazing mouths, time itself declaring 'I am become Death, destroyer of worlds.' Arjuna, terrified, begs for the familiar face back. Some truth can be witnessed only briefly; love restores the human scale in which we can live.
reflections: Arjuna learns the warriors are 'already slain' — he is only the instrument. Where might you be an instrument of something already in motion?
What truth have you glimpsed that was too large to hold — and what did you do with it?
Why do you think the vision ends with a request for the gentle, familiar form?

name: भक्तियोग
teaching: Between the formless absolute and the beloved with a face, Krishna gently favours the face — not because the formless is false, but because the embodied heart needs somewhere to rest. And if love is hard, practice; if practice is hard, work as offering; if even that is hard, simply let go of results. The chapter ends not with metaphysics but with a portrait of the devotee: friendly, compassionate, free of malice, the same in honour and disgrace.
reflections: Which rung of Krishna's ladder — absorption, practice, offered work, surrendered results — is actually within your reach this week?
Of the devotee's qualities listed at the end, which one is hardest for you?
Whom do you find easy to love — and is your spiritual life connected to that ease, or separate from it?

name: क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोग
teaching: The body, the mind, desire, aversion, intelligence — all of it is the field. You are the knower of the field. Liberation begins with this clean distinction: whatever can be watched is not the watcher. The same light knows every field, undivided, like one sun illumining the whole world.
reflections: List what you call 'me' — body, moods, history, opinions. Which of these can you observe? What is left?
Krishna defines knowledge as humility, harmlessness, steadiness. Where has your knowledge made you less humble?
Can you spend one difficult moment today as the field's witness rather than its owner?

name: गुणत्रयविभागयोग
teaching: All of nature is woven of three strands: sattva binds with clarity and the pride of knowing, rajas with craving and restlessness, tamas with heaviness and sleep. Even goodness is a golden chain. Freedom is not perfecting the weave but seeing the weave — knowing the guṇas move among the guṇas while the witness stands apart.
reflections: Which strand dominated your last twenty-four hours — clarity, agitation, or dullness?
How does your sattva bind you — what subtle pride rides on your discipline or insight?
The one beyond the guṇas 'neither hates their presence nor longs for their absence.' Can you let today's mood simply move through?

name: पुरुषोत्तमयोग
teaching: The world is an aśvattha tree grown upside down — roots above in the unseen, branches below nourished by the guṇas, and no end of it visible from inside. Krishna's instruction is severe and brief: cut it with the axe of non-attachment, then seek the place from which no one returns. A fragment of the divine lives as every soul; the whole exceeds both the perishable and the imperishable.
reflections: What are the deepest roots of your world-tree — the assumptions everything else branches from?
Non-attachment is called an axe, not a mood. What would one deliberate cut look like?
If a fragment of the supreme is living as you, how does that change what self-respect means?

name: दैवासुरसम्पद्विभागयोग
teaching: Two inheritances run through every human heart: one of fearlessness, charity, and harmlessness; one of hypocrisy, arrogance, and the creed that the world is loveless and false, with desire its only law. Krishna names desire, anger, and greed as the threefold gate of self-destruction — and scripture as the lamp for telling the two inheritances apart when they speak in your own voice.
reflections: Which 'demonic' trait do you recognise not in others but in yourself, even faintly?
Desire, anger, greed — which gate stands open widest in your life right now?
When your impulses and your principles conflict, what do you actually consult?

name: श्रद्धात्रयविभागयोग
teaching: Your faith is not what you profess — it is what you are: 'a person is made of their śraddhā.' Food, worship, austerity, giving — each splits three ways by the strand behind it, and the same outward act can purify or poison depending on its spirit. The chapter ends with oṁ tat sat: the seal of the real on anything done with faith, and the verdict — asat, nothing — on anything done without it.
reflections: Watch one ordinary choice today — a meal, a gift, a discipline. Which of the three faiths chose it?
Where do you practice austerity for the sake of being seen?
What in your life is done entirely without faith — and is it, as the chapter says, nothing?

name: मोक्षसंन्यासयोग
teaching: The whole Gita returns in a final spiral: act, but release the fruit; honour your own dharma, even done imperfectly, over another's done well; let devotion carry what effort cannot. Then the teaching ends as all true teaching must — 'thus I have declared it; reflect fully, and do as you choose.' And when every support fails, the last word: abandon all dharmas, take refuge in Me alone; do not grieve.
reflections: What is your svadharma — the work that is yours even when done imperfectly?
Krishna ends with 'do as you choose.' What does it mean that the Gita's last move is to return your freedom?
Having walked all eighteen chapters: what, now, will you actually do — like Arjuna, whose final words are 'my doubt is gone; I will act'?
